title: David Hidalgo

infobox.value: David Kent Hidalgo   (1954-10-06) October 6, 1954 (age 71)  [Los Angeles, California](/wiki/Los_Angeles,_California "Los Angeles, California"), U.S.

* Vocals * guitar * [accordion](/wiki/Accordion "Accordion") * violin * [6-string banjo](/wiki/6-string_banjo "6-string banjo") * cello * [requinto jarocho](/wiki/Requinto_jarocho "Requinto jarocho") * percussion * drums

from the article and its talk page, especially if potentially [libelous](/wiki/Wikipedia:Libel "Wikipedia:Libel"). *Find sources:* ["David Hidalgo"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22David+Hidalgo%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22David+Hidalgo%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22David+Hidalgo%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22David+Hidalgo%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22David+Hidalgo%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22David+Hidalgo%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(October 2023)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

(born October 6, 1954)[[1]](#cite_note-1) is an American singer-songwriter, best known for his work with the band [Los Lobos](/wiki/Los_Lobos "Los Lobos"). Hidalgo frequently plays [accordion](/wiki/Accordion "Accordion"), violin, [6-string banjo](/wiki/6-string_banjo "6-string banjo"), [cello](/wiki/Cello "Cello"), [requinto jarocho](/wiki/Requinto_jarocho "Requinto jarocho"), percussion, drums and [guitar](/wiki/Guitar "Guitar") as a [session musician](/wiki/Session_musician "Session musician") on other artists' releases.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American musician

This article is about the Los Lobos vocalist. For his son, see [David Hidalgo Jr.](/wiki/David_Hidalgo_Jr. "David Hidalgo Jr.")

Musical artist

**David Kent Hidalgo** (born October 6, 1954)[[1]](#cite_note-1) is an American singer-songwriter, best known for his work with the band [Los Lobos](/wiki/Los_Lobos "Los Lobos"). Hidalgo frequently plays [accordion](/wiki/Accordion "Accordion"), violin, [6-string banjo](/wiki/6-string_banjo "6-string banjo"), [cello](/wiki/Cello "Cello"), [requinto jarocho](/wiki/Requinto_jarocho "Requinto jarocho"), percussion, drums and [guitar](/wiki/Guitar "Guitar") as a [session musician](/wiki/Session_musician "Session musician") on other artists' releases.

## Early life and education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=David_Hidalgo&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life and education")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [[icon]](/wiki/File:Wiki_letter_w_cropped.svg) | **This section is empty.** You can help by [adding to it](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=David_Hidalgo&action=edit&section=). *(September 2019)* |

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=David_Hidalgo&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

In 1973, Hidalgo was one of the founding members of [Los Lobos](/wiki/Los_Lobos "Los Lobos"), for which he wrote most songs together with Louie Pérez. He also participated as a guest musician on albums of other artists, among them David Alvin, [Buckwheat Zydeco](/wiki/Buckwheat_Zydeco "Buckwheat Zydeco"), [Paul Burlison](/wiki/Paul_Burlison "Paul Burlison"), [T-Bone Burnett](/wiki/T-Bone_Burnett "T-Bone Burnett"), [Peter Case](/wiki/Peter_Case "Peter Case"), [Toni Childs](/wiki/Toni_Childs "Toni Childs"), [Marc Cohn](/wiki/Marc_Cohn "Marc Cohn"), [Ry Cooder](/wiki/Ry_Cooder "Ry Cooder"), [Elvis Costello](/wiki/Elvis_Costello "Elvis Costello"), [Crowded House](/wiki/Crowded_House "Crowded House"), [The Fabulous Thunderbirds](/wiki/The_Fabulous_Thunderbirds "The Fabulous Thunderbirds"), [John Lee Hooker](/wiki/John_Lee_Hooker "John Lee Hooker"), [Rickie Lee Jones](/wiki/Rickie_Lee_Jones "Rickie Lee Jones"), [Leo Kottke](/wiki/Leo_Kottke "Leo Kottke"), [Roy Orbison](/wiki/Roy_Orbison "Roy Orbison"), [Dolly Parton](/wiki/Dolly_Parton "Dolly Parton"), [Pierce Pettis](/wiki/Pierce_Pettis "Pierce Pettis"), [Bonnie Raitt](/wiki/Bonnie_Raitt "Bonnie Raitt"), [Paul Simon](/wiki/Paul_Simon "Paul Simon"), [Taj Mahal](/wiki/Taj_Mahal_(musician) "Taj Mahal (musician)"), [Suzanne Vega](/wiki/Suzanne_Vega "Suzanne Vega"), [Bob Dylan](/wiki/Bob_Dylan "Bob Dylan") and [Tom Waits](/wiki/Tom_Waits "Tom Waits"). He is a member of the [supergroup](/wiki/Supergroup_(music) "Supergroup (music)") [Los Super Seven](/wiki/Los_Super_Seven "Los Super Seven") and of the [Latin Playboys](/wiki/Latin_Playboys "Latin Playboys"), a side project made up of some of the members of Los Lobos. With Mike Halby of [Canned Heat](/wiki/Canned_Heat "Canned Heat"), he formed another band, Houndog, as a side project. He also appeared on national television in the U.S., backing Waits.

In 1987, he contributed the song "Will the Wolf Survive?" to the movie *Promised Land*. For [Dennis Hopper](/wiki/Dennis_Hopper "Dennis Hopper")'s *[Colors](/wiki/Colors_(1988_film) "Colors (1988 film)")* (1988), he wrote the song "One Time, One Night". He wrote the songs "Manifold De Amour", "Forever Night Shade Mary" and "Chinese Surprize" for the 1995 action film *[Desperado](/wiki/Desperado_(film) "Desperado (film)").* He contributed his melancholic song "La pistola y el corazón" for the movie *[The Mexican](/wiki/The_Mexican_(film) "The Mexican (film)")* (2001).

Hidalgo's songs have been [covered](/wiki/Cover_version "Cover version") by the [Jerry Garcia Band](/wiki/Jerry_Garcia_Band "Jerry Garcia Band"), [Waylon Jennings](/wiki/Waylon_Jennings "Waylon Jennings"), [Bonnie Raitt](/wiki/Bonnie_Raitt "Bonnie Raitt") and others. He has performed in [Eric Clapton](/wiki/Eric_Clapton "Eric Clapton")'s [Crossroads Guitar Festival](/wiki/Crossroads_Guitar_Festival "Crossroads Guitar Festival") since its inception in 2004, including a performance with Los Lobos in April 2013 at Madison Square Garden. Clapton joined the band on stage for their song "Burn It Down", from their 2010 album, *[Tin Can Trust](/wiki/Tin_Can_Trust "Tin Can Trust")*.

## Personal life and family

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=David_Hidalgo&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Personal life and family")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [[icon]](/wiki/File:Wiki_letter_w_cropped.svg) | This section **needs expansion**. You can help by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=David_Hidalgo&action=edit&section=). *(September 2019)* |

His son, [David Jr.](/wiki/David_Hidalgo_Jr. "David Hidalgo Jr."), is the drummer for the band [Social Distortion](/wiki/Social_Distortion "Social Distortion"). His other son Vincent has played bass for the band [Mariachi El Bronx](/wiki/The_Bronx_(band) "The Bronx (band)").

## Discography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=David_Hidalgo&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Discography")]

### Selected collaborations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=David_Hidalgo&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Selected collaborations")]

* [T-Bone Burnett](/wiki/T-Bone_Burnett "T-Bone Burnett") (guitar, accordion, vocals, 8-String Bass on *[T-Bone Burnett](/wiki/T-Bone_Burnett_(album) "T-Bone Burnett (album)")* (1986)
* [Peter Case](/wiki/Peter_Case "Peter Case") (*The Man with the Blue Post Modern Fragmented Neo-Traditionalist Guitar*)
* [Los Cenzontles](/wiki/Los_Cenzontles "Los Cenzontles") (co-producer, songwriter and multi-instrumentalist on *[Songs of Wood & Steel](/w/index.php?title=Songs_of_Wood_%26_Steel&action=edit&redlink=1 "Songs of Wood & Steel (page does not exist)")*)
* [Eric Clapton](/wiki/Eric_Clapton "Eric Clapton") (Crossroads festival DVD, CD)
* [Ry Cooder](/wiki/Ry_Cooder "Ry Cooder") (*[Chávez Ravine](/wiki/Ch%C3%A1vez_Ravine_(album) "Chávez Ravine (album)")*)
* [Elvis Costello](/wiki/Elvis_Costello "Elvis Costello") (harmony vocal on *[King of America](/wiki/King_of_America "King of America")*, guitar and harmony vocal on *[Momofuku](/wiki/Momofuku_(album) "Momofuku (album)")*)
* [Toni Childs](/wiki/Toni_Childs "Toni Childs") (*[House of Hope](/wiki/House_of_Hope_(album) "House of Hope (album)")*)
* [Crowded House](/wiki/Crowded_House "Crowded House") (accordion "As Sure as I Am" on *[Woodface](/wiki/Woodface "Woodface")* (1991))
* [Bob Dylan](/wiki/Bob_Dylan "Bob Dylan") (accordion on *[Together Through Life](/wiki/Together_Through_Life "Together Through Life")* and *[Christmas in the Heart](/wiki/Christmas_in_the_Heart "Christmas in the Heart")*; guitar, accordion, violin on *[Tempest](/wiki/Tempest_(Bob_Dylan_album) "Tempest (Bob Dylan album)")*)
* [John Hammond](/wiki/John_P._Hammond "John P. Hammond") (guitar, mandolin, vocals on *[Ready for love](/w/index.php?title=Ready_for_love&action=edit&redlink=1 "Ready for love (page does not exist)")* (2003))
* [John Lee Hooker](/wiki/John_Lee_Hooker "John Lee Hooker") (multiple projects)
* [Roy Orbison](/wiki/Roy_Orbison "Roy Orbison") (*[King of Hearts](/wiki/King_of_Hearts_(Roy_Orbison_album) "King of Hearts (Roy Orbison album)")*)
* [Willy DeVille](/wiki/Willy_DeVille "Willy DeVille") (*[Backstreets of Desire](/wiki/Backstreets_of_Desire "Backstreets of Desire")*, *[Crow Jane Alley](/wiki/Crow_Jane_Alley "Crow Jane Alley")*)
* [Gov't Mule](/wiki/Gov%27t_Mule "Gov't Mule") (Politician) (guitar and vocals on *[The Deepest End, Live in Concert](/wiki/The_Deepest_End,_Live_in_Concert "The Deepest End, Live in Concert")*)
* The 1994 tribute to songwriter [Mark Heard](/wiki/Mark_Heard "Mark Heard"), *[Strong Hand of Love](/wiki/Strong_Hand_of_Love "Strong Hand of Love")*
* [Mato Nanji](/wiki/Indigenous_(band) "Indigenous (band)")
* [Luther Dickinson](/wiki/Luther_Dickinson "Luther Dickinson") (*3 Skulls and the Truth*, [Blues Bureau International](/wiki/Blues_Bureau_International "Blues Bureau International"))
* [Rickie Lee Jones](/wiki/Rickie_Lee_Jones "Rickie Lee Jones") (*[Traffic from Paradise](/wiki/Traffic_from_Paradise "Traffic from Paradise")*, *[The Evening of My Best Day](/wiki/The_Evening_of_My_Best_Day "The Evening of My Best Day")*)
* [Tonio K.](/wiki/Tonio_K. "Tonio K.") (*Olé*)
* [Leo Kottke](/wiki/Leo_Kottke "Leo Kottke") (*[Try and Stop Me](/wiki/Try_and_Stop_Me "Try and Stop Me")*)
* [G. Love & Special Sauce](/wiki/G._Love_%26_Special_Sauce "G. Love & Special Sauce") (viola on "Missing My Baby")
* [Taj Mahal](/wiki/Taj_Mahal_(musician) "Taj Mahal (musician)") & [Los Cenzontles](/wiki/Los_Cenzontles "Los Cenzontles") (co-producer, songwriter and multi-instrumentalist on "American Horizon")
* [Ozomatli](/wiki/Ozomatli "Ozomatli") (multiple projects)
* [Dolly Parton](/wiki/Dolly_Parton "Dolly Parton") (*[Treasures](/wiki/Treasures_(Dolly_Parton_album) "Treasures (Dolly Parton album)")*)
* [Pierce Pettis](/wiki/Pierce_Pettis "Pierce Pettis") (*Chase the Buffalo*)
* [Bonnie Raitt](/wiki/Bonnie_Raitt "Bonnie Raitt") (*[Fundamental](/wiki/Fundamental_(Bonnie_Raitt_album) "Fundamental (Bonnie Raitt album)")*)
* [Marc Ribot](/wiki/Marc_Ribot "Marc Ribot") (*Border Music*)
* [Paul Simon](/wiki/Paul_Simon "Paul Simon") ("All Around the World or The Myth of Fingerprints" on *[Graceland](/wiki/Graceland_(album) "Graceland (album)")* (1986))
* [Martin Simpson](/wiki/Martin_Simpson "Martin Simpson") (*Kambara Music in Native Tongues* (1998))
* [Tremoloco](/w/index.php?title=Tremoloco&action=edit&redlink=1 "Tremoloco (page does not exist)") (Dulcinea)
* [Suzanne Vega](/wiki/Suzanne_Vega "Suzanne Vega") (*[99.9F°](/wiki/99.9F%C2%B0 "99.9F°")*)
* [Tom Waits](/wiki/Tom_Waits "Tom Waits") (accordion on "Cold, Cold Ground" and "Train Song" on *[Frank's Wild Years](/wiki/Franks_Wild_Years "Franks Wild Years")* (1987); accordion and violin on "Whistle Down the Wind" on *[Bone Machine](/wiki/Bone_Machine "Bone Machine")* (1992); guitar, accordion, violin, bass, backing vocals on several tracks on *[Bad As Me](/wiki/Bad_as_Me "Bad as Me")* (2011))
* [Buckwheat Zydeco](/wiki/Buckwheat_Zydeco "Buckwheat Zydeco") (multiple projects)
